title: Pendor Gyrr

{{Quote|Stopping her is very important to the future security of the New Republic's currency.|General Airen Cracken, on Pendor Gyrr|<ref name="WBC 33" />}}
'''Pendor Gyrr''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Givin/Legends|Givin]] criminal active during the [[Time/Legends|days]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Although she tried her hand with [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] and [[Black market/Legends|black market]] sales, she made her fortune by [[Forger (profession)/Legends|counterfeiting]] [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Gyrr employed her natural Givin talent for mathematics to create false [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] and New Republic currency, with which she then purchased goods. Although her operation alone did not pose a threat to the stability of the New Republic credit, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] posted a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Gyrr to prevent her from inspiring other criminals to follow her lead. She was believed to have several bases on [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Pendor seems to have profited immensely from the economic instability the galaxy now faces.|Airen Cracken|<ref name="WBC 33" />}}
Born on her [[Species/Legends|species]]' [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Yag'Dhul/Legends|Yag'Dhul]] in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], the [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Givin/Legends|Givin]] Pendor Gyrr found herself ill at ease among her own people. She instead left the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] and made a name for herself as a [[Forger (profession)/Legends|currency forger]]. After the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] destabilized [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Gyrr capitalized on the economic turmoil to create vast amounts of false currency, including both [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Gyr played the [[Galactic economy|financial markets]] by waiting until the value of a particular currency was high, then using her fake credits to make purchases. One of her employees was [[Qan Gindoch]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]], until he was captured on the planet [[Mantooine/Legends|Mantooine]] by agents of the New Republic, who suspected him of involvement in the counterfeiting operation. Gindoch confessed all he knew and was released in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]]. Gindoch's testimony helped the Republic link confiscated counterfeit money to Gyrr's organization, and thus detect telltale imperfections in her forgeries.<ref name="WBC 33" />

Gyrr supplemented her operations by selling stolen items on the [[Black market/Legends|black market]] and smuggling [[Spice/Legends|spice]]. The New Republic [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] believed that Gyrr had bases at several [[Merchant/Legends|trading]] planets and [[Star system/Legends|systems]], including [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], [[Celanon/Legends|Celanon]], the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]], [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], and [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]].<ref name="WBC 33" />

Although Gyrr's forgery racket was small enough to spare the New Republic devaluation of its currency, the [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government's inability to stop her operation worried Cracken that other criminals would follow her example and compound the problem. In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Cracken accordingly put out a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of 15,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for the Givin's capture. Cracken noted unconfirmed reports that suggested the Givin's whereabouts at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the posting were on Ord Mantell.<ref name="WBC 33" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|While Pendor Gyrr is a bit of a misfit on her native Yag'Dhul, she has made a fortune by applying her species' natural aptitude for mathematics to more 'practical' uses: counterfeiting.|Airen Cracken|<ref name="WBC 33" />}}
Pendor Gyrr used the mathematical talents of her Givin heritage as a boon in creating her false currency, a skill at which she was an expert—despite imperfections that allowed a skilled analyst to detect her handiwork. She was a competent businessbeing, but she was especially knowledgeable of banking procedures, currencies, and financial markets. She was also able to get along in criminal circles. Nevertheless, Gyrr did not fit in with Givin society and instead preferred life off Yag'Dhul. Gyrr was a [[Hair/Legends|hairless]], [[Color/Legends|black]]-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]], 1.6-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-tall Givin<ref name="WBC 33" /> with the [[Skeleton/Legends|bone]]-white [[exoskeleton]] characteristic of her species.<ref name="GG4b 44" />

==Equipment==
Gyrr commonly carried a hand [[scanner]], plates for credits, a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], and a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]]. In an image that accompanied her New Republic bounty profile, Gyrr wore a loose-fitting tunic–trousers combination and sandals.<ref name="WBC 33" />

==Behind the scenes==
Gyrr is featured in the [[Roleplaying game|role-playing]] sourcebook [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] and published by [[West End Games]] in [[1993]]. The illustration of Gyrr that accompanies the entry was drawn by [[Rob Caswell]].<ref name="WBC 33" />

==Sources==
